description: Support multiple concurrent WebSocket connections from the Chrome extension (one per browser profile). Currently the MCP server holds a single extensionWs slot — when both regular and incognito extension instances connect, they enter an infinite reconnection loop, each evicting the other every ~1 second. After this work, the server tracks multiple connections keyed by a connection ID, scopes tab mappings and dispatches per-connection, and broadcasts notifications to all connections. The extension sends a profile identifier on connect so the server can distinguish connections.

userStories.title: Add ExtensionConnection type and replace single extensionWs with a connections Map
userStories.description: Replace the single `extensionWs: WsHandle | null` field on ServerState with a `Map<string, ExtensionConnection>` that holds multiple connections keyed by connection ID. Add an ExtensionConnection interface. Update createState() to initialize the new field. Remove the old extensionWs field.
acceptanceCriteria: ServerState has `extensionConnections: Map<string, ExtensionConnection>` instead of `extensionWs`
ExtensionConnection holds ws, connectionId, and tabMapping (Map<string, TabMapping>)
activeNetworkCaptures is scoped per-connection inside ExtensionConnection
createState() initializes extensionConnections as an empty Map
No references to `extensionWs` remain in state.ts
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts (modify ServerState interface, createState, add ExtensionConnection type)

Context:
The current single-connection model at state.ts:273 is `extensionWs: WsHandle | null`. Tab mapping at state.ts:267 is `tabMapping: Map<string, TabMapping>`. Network captures at state.ts:312 is `activeNetworkCaptures: Set<number>`. These three fields must be scoped per-connection.

Changes:
1. Add new interface:
```typescript
export interface ExtensionConnection {
  ws: WsHandle;
  connectionId: string;
  /** Tab-to-plugin mapping for this connection */
  tabMapping: Map<string, TabMapping>;
  /** Tab IDs with active network capture for this connection */
  activeNetworkCaptures: Set<number>;
}
```

2. Replace `extensionWs: WsHandle | null` (line 273) with `extensionConnections: Map<string, ExtensionConnection>`

3. Remove `tabMapping: Map<string, TabMapping>` from ServerState (line 267) — it moves into ExtensionConnection

4. Remove `activeNetworkCaptures: Set<number>` from ServerState (line 312) — it moves into ExtensionConnection

5. Update createState() (line 332-370): replace `extensionWs: null` with `extensionConnections: new Map()`, remove `tabMapping: new Map()` and `activeNetworkCaptures: new Set()`

6. Add helper functions:
- `getAnyConnection(state): ExtensionConnection | undefined` — returns any active connection (for backwards compat during migration)
- `getConnectionForTab(state, tabId): ExtensionConnection | undefined` — finds which connection owns a given tabId by scanning all connections' tabMappings
- `getMergedTabMapping(state): Map<string, TabMapping>` — merges all connections' tabMappings into one (for plugin_list_tabs and health endpoint)
- `isExtensionConnected(state): boolean` — returns `state.extensionConnections.size > 0`

7. Bump STATE_SCHEMA_VERSION (currently 5 at line 318) to 6

Constraints:
- PendingDispatch (line 131-146) does NOT need a connectionId field yet — that comes in US-003
- Do NOT add `pendingConfirmations` to ExtensionConnection — keep it on ServerState since confirmations are not tab-scoped
- Do NOT touch any other file in this story — only state.ts. Compilation will break until subsequent stories update consumers.

userStories.title: Update sendToExtension to broadcast and add sendToConnection for targeted sends
userStories.description: Refactor sendToExtension in extension-handlers.ts to broadcast messages to all active connections. Add a new sendToConnection function for sending to a specific connection. Update all notification senders (sendSyncFull, sendInvocationStart/End, sendExtensionReload, sendPluginUpdate, sendConfirmationRequest) to use the correct send path.
acceptanceCriteria: sendToExtension broadcasts to all connections in state.extensionConnections
sendToConnection sends to a specific connection by ID
sendSyncFull sends to all connections
sendExtensionReload sends to all connections
sendInvocationStart/End sends to all connections
sendConfirmationRequest sends to all connections (confirmation UI may show in any side panel)

userStories.title: Update dispatchToExtension to route dispatches to the correct connection
userStories.description: Modify dispatchToExtension to resolve which connection owns the target tab and send the dispatch over that specific connection. For dispatches with a tabId, look up which connection owns that tab. For dispatches without tabId, use the merged tab mapping to auto-select, then route to the owning connection. Add connectionId to PendingDispatch for scoped disconnect cleanup.
acceptanceCriteria: dispatchToExtension resolves the correct connection for targeted dispatches (with tabId)
dispatchToExtension resolves the correct connection for auto-select dispatches (without tabId)
PendingDispatch includes a connectionId field
Dispatch rejects with 'Extension not connected' when no connections exist
Dispatch rejects with 'Tab not found in any connection' when tabId doesn't match any connection's tabMapping

userStories.title: Update WebSocket open/close handlers and tab sync for multi-connection
userStories.description: Rewrite createHandleWsOpen to add new connections to the Map instead of replacing. Rewrite createHandleWsClose to remove only that connection's state. Update handleTabSyncAll and handleTabStateChanged to scope tab mappings per-connection. Parse the connection ID from the WebSocket subprotocol header.
acceptanceCriteria: New WebSocket connections are added to extensionConnections Map keyed by connection ID
Closing a connection removes only that connection's entry and its tab mappings
Closing a connection only rejects pending dispatches that were sent over that connection
handleTabSyncAll updates only the sender connection's tabMapping, not all connections
handleTabStateChanged updates only the sender connection's tabMapping
Connection ID is parsed from the Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header (format: 'opentabs, <secret>, <connectionId>')
Connection ID falls back to a random UUID if not provided (backwards compat)

userStories.title: Update health endpoint, mcp-tool-dispatch, and reload to use multi-connection state
userStories.description: Update all remaining consumers of the old extensionWs field to use the new multi-connection state. This includes the health endpoint, mcp-tool-dispatch.ts, reload.ts, and any other files that reference extensionWs.
acceptanceCriteria: Health endpoint returns extensionConnected: true when any connection exists
Health endpoint returns extensionConnections count
handlePluginToolCall checks extensionConnections.size > 0 instead of extensionWs
reloadCore sends sync.full to all connections
performReload sends extension reload to all connections
plugin_list_tabs returns merged tab mapping from all connections
No references to `extensionWs` remain in the codebase (except tests and this migration)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts (health endpoint)
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts (handlePluginToolCall)
- platform/mcp-server/src/reload.ts (reloadCore, performReload, file watcher callbacks)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (buildConfigStatePayload uses state.tabMapping)

Context:
All remaining references to `state.extensionWs`:

1. http-routes.ts:327: `extensionConnected: state.extensionWs !== null` in health endpoint
   → Change to: `extensionConnected: state.extensionConnections.size > 0`
   → Add: `extensionConnections: state.extensionConnections.size`

2. http-routes.ts:410: `if (!state.extensionWs)` in handleExtensionReload endpoint
   → Change to: `if (state.extensionConnections.size === 0)`

3. mcp-tool-dispatch.ts:383: `if (!state.extensionWs)` early exit
   → Change to: `if (state.extensionConnections.size === 0)` or use `isExtensionConnected(state)`

4. reload.ts:333: `if (state.extensionWs)` before sendSyncFull
   → Change to: `if (state.extensionConnections.size > 0)`

5. reload.ts:184-188: `if (state.extensionWs)` in file watcher callback
   → Same change

6. reload.ts:415: `if (state.extensionWs)` in performReload
   → Same change

7. reload.ts:421: `state.pendingExtensionReload = true` when extension not connected
   → Keep this on ServerState (not per-connection) — it defers reload until ANY connection is made

8. buildConfigStatePayload (extension-handlers.ts:306) reads `state.tabMapping.get(p.name)` for each plugin
   → Change to use getMergedTabMapping(state) helper from US-001

9. plugin_list_tabs browser tool reads from state.tabMapping
   → Find where this is implemented and update to use getMergedTabMapping

Search for all `state.tabMapping` references outside of handleTabSyncAll/handleTabStateChanged — these all need to use the merged view.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove extensionWs from the WsHandle type in shared/ — that's a different thing (the interface for WebSocket handles, not the state field)
- The health endpoint response shape should be backwards-compatible — `extensionConnected` stays as a boolean. Add `extensionConnections` as a new field.

userStories.title: Extension: send connection ID in WebSocket subprotocol header
userStories.description: Update the Chrome extension's offscreen document to send a stable connection ID as a third Sec-WebSocket-Protocol value when connecting to the MCP server. The ID is generated once per extension installation and stored in chrome.storage.local. This lets the server distinguish connections from different browser profiles.
acceptanceCriteria: A stable connectionId is generated on first install and stored in chrome.storage.local
The WebSocket connects with protocols ['opentabs', '<secret>', '<connectionId>']
The connectionId persists across extension reloads and browser restarts
The connectionId is unique per browser profile (regular vs incognito get different IDs since chrome.storage.local is isolated per profile)

userStories.title: Update unit tests for multi-connection server state
userStories.description: Update all unit tests that mock or reference extensionWs, tabMapping, or activeNetworkCaptures to use the new multi-connection structure. Tests should verify per-connection scoping, broadcasting, and connection-aware dispatch routing.
acceptanceCriteria: All tests in extension-protocol.test.ts pass with the new multi-connection state
All tests in extension-handlers.test.ts pass
All tests in state.test.ts pass
All tests in http-routes.test.ts pass
All tests in mcp-tool-dispatch.test.ts pass
New tests verify: two connections can coexist, closing one doesn't affect the other, dispatches route to the correct connection, tab.syncAll scopes per-connection
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.test.ts (update extensionWs mocks)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.test.ts (update tabMapping, sendToExtension mocks)
- platform/mcp-server/src/state.test.ts (update createState expectations)
- platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.test.ts (update health endpoint extensionConnected tests)
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.test.ts (update extensionWs checks)
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup-dispatch.test.ts (if it references extensionWs)

Context:
The existing tests mock `state.extensionWs` directly. For example, extension-protocol.test.ts sets up:
```typescript
state.extensionWs = { send: vi.fn(), close: vi.fn() };
```
This must change to:
```typescript
const conn = { ws: { send: vi.fn(), close: vi.fn() }, connectionId: 'test-conn', tabMapping: new Map(), activeNetworkCaptures: new Set() };
state.extensionConnections.set('test-conn', conn);
```

http-routes.test.ts:290 checks `expect(body.extensionConnected).toBe(false)` — this should still pass since no connections exist by default.

Changes:
1. Create a test helper `createMockConnection(id?: string): ExtensionConnection` that returns a mock connection with vi.fn() send/close.

2. Update all `state.extensionWs = mockWs` to `state.extensionConnections.set(id, createMockConnection(id))`.

3. Update all `state.extensionWs = null` to `state.extensionConnections.clear()`.

4. Update all `state.tabMapping.set(...)` to set on the connection's tabMapping instead: `conn.tabMapping.set(...)`.

5. Add new test cases:
   a. Two connections coexist — sendToExtension broadcasts to both
   b. Closing connection A doesn't reject dispatches on connection B
   c. handleTabSyncAll from connection A doesn't clear connection B's tabs
   d. dispatchToExtension routes to the connection owning the target tabId
   e. When tabId matches no connection, dispatch falls back to first available connection
   f. getMergedTabMapping correctly merges tabs from multiple connections
   g. Health endpoint shows extensionConnected: true when any connection exists

Constraints:
- Do NOT change test logic for things unrelated to multi-connection (e.g., permission tests, plugin discovery tests)
- Test the backwards-compatible behavior: single connection should work exactly as before
- Mock the ws.data or connectionId extraction as needed for the open handler tests

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for multi-connection WebSocket support
userStories.description: Add comprehensive E2E tests that verify multiple WebSocket connections can coexist, tab state is scoped per-connection, dispatches route correctly, and disconnecting one connection doesn't affect the other. Simulate multiple connections using raw WebSocket clients (similar to dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts patterns).
acceptanceCriteria: Test: two WebSocket connections with different connectionIds coexist without eviction
Test: health endpoint shows extensionConnected: true with two connections
Test: closing one connection doesn't reject pending dispatches on the other
Test: tab.syncAll from one connection doesn't affect the other connection's tabs
Test: reconnecting with the same connectionId replaces only that connection
Test: connection without connectionId gets a random UUID (backwards compat)
All E2E tests pass including existing ones (no regressions)
npm run test:e2e passes
